title: tragiola pilosa subsp. epilis
intro: Tragiola pilosa subsp. epilis is a subspecies of plant, known as a type of wild chamomile or small chamomile, belonging to the family Asteraceae. The medicinal properties of Tragiola pilosa subsp. epilis include its anti-inflammatory and calming effects, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to promote relaxation, reduce anxiety and stress, and aid in improving sleep quality. Tragiola pilosa subsp. epilis can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional medicine to soothe digestive issues and calm the nervous system.
